---
title: "nitrix.metrics.classification"
generated: true
signature-hash: 26d886bcc6597993
description: "Classification / segmentation comparison kernels."
listing:
  id: nitrix-metrics-classification-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-metrics-classification/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.metrics.classification`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Classification / segmentation comparison kernels.

*Cross-entropy losses* (costs, minimise; `reduction` selects
`"mean"` / `"sum"` / `"none"`):

- [`bce_with_logits`](nitrix-metrics-classification/bce_with_logits.qmd) -- binary cross-entropy from logits, in the
  numerically stable $\max(x, 0) - x t + \log(1 + e^{-|x|})$ form
  (no intermediate `sigmoid` / `log` that overflows for large
  $|x|$).
- [`cross_entropy_with_logits`](nitrix-metrics-classification/cross_entropy_with_logits.qmd) -- categorical cross-entropy from
  logits against integer class targets (`log_softmax` + gather).
- [`focal_loss`](nitrix-metrics-classification/focal_loss.qmd) -- the focal loss of Lin et al. (2017): binary
  cross-entropy down-weighted by $(1 - p_t)^{\gamma}$ so easy,
  well-classified examples contribute little, with an optional
  class-balancing $\alpha$.  Shares the stable
  binary-cross-entropy core with [`bce_with_logits`](nitrix-metrics-classification/bce_with_logits.qmd).

*Evaluation metrics* (reporting, not training objectives; **not
differentiable** -- they ride argsort / bincount / top-`k` operations,
which are piecewise-constant and so carry no useful gradient):

- [`roc_auc`](nitrix-metrics-classification/roc_auc.qmd) -- area under the ROC curve via the rank
  (Mann-Whitney $U$) form, so ties are tie-corrected by average
  ranks.  A **global** statistic: score it on the *gathered*
  `(scores, labels)`, never as a per-batch mean (averaging AUCs is not
  the AUC).
- [`confusion_matrix`](nitrix-metrics-classification/confusion_matrix.qmd) -- integer count matrix, `row = true` /
  `col = predicted` (the sklearn orientation).
- [`topk_accuracy`](nitrix-metrics-classification/topk_accuracy.qmd) -- fraction of rows whose top-`k` predictions
  contain the true label.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### References

.. [1] Lin, T.-Y., Goyal, P., Girshick, R., He, K., & Dollár, P. (2017).
   Focal loss for dense object detection. *Proceedings of the IEEE
   International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)*, 2980-2988.
   `10.1109/ICCV.2017.324`
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `C` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-metrics-classification-symbols}
:::
